---
title: ""
---

```{=html}
<video autoplay muted loop playsinline 
         style="width: 100%; max-width: 1000px; height: auto; display: block; border: none;">
```

<source src="experience.mp4" type="video/mp4">

</video>

# Education

-   **Ph.D., Public Health (Epidemiology)**\
    Florida International University

-   **Master of Public Health (MPH), Epidemiology**\
    Florida International University

-   **Bachelor of Science in Public Health**\
    University of South Florida

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Experience

## Assistant Professor

**Morehouse School of Medicine**\
*Current*

I lead applied work at the intersection of epidemiology, data science, and public health, combining real-world data with advanced analytics to generate actionable insights. My role integrates teaching, research, and hands-on data analysis using modern statistical and computational approaches.

**Core Skills Demonstrated:**

-   Applied statistical modeling and predictive analytics using large-scale health datasets\
-   Machine learning applications in public health and clinical research\
-   Analysis of real-world data, including EHR (Epic, Cerner), survey, and clinical datasets\
-   Design of reproducible data workflows using R, Quarto, and GitHub\
-   Translation of analytic findings into actionable insights for public health and research

**Key Contributions:**

-   Designed and taught graduate-level courses in Epidemiology, Biostatistics, and Health Informatics with a strong emphasis on applied data science\
-   Developed hands-on curriculum using real-world datasets to train students in statistical programming and data analysis\
-   Led research projects focused on cancer screening behaviors, clinical trial participation, and health disparities\
-   Mentored students in data analysis, manuscript development, and reproducible research practices

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Research and Data Science Experience

Across my academic and research roles, I have consistently applied data-driven approaches to answer complex public health questions, working with diverse datasets and analytic methods.

**Technical and Analytical Experience:**

-   Built regression and predictive models (linear, logistic, Poisson) to evaluate health outcomes and risk factors\
-   Conducted survey-weighted analyses using large national datasets (e.g., BRFSS)\
-   Applied machine learning techniques including decision trees, clustering, and classification methods\
-   Performed data cleaning, transformation, and feature engineering for large, complex datasets\
-   Developed reproducible analysis pipelines and reports using Quarto and version control

**Data Environments and Sources:**

-   Electronic Health Records (Epic, Cerner)\
-   Clinical and observational datasets\
-   National survey datasets (e.g., BRFSS, NHANES)\
-   Public health surveillance data

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Selected Institutional Experience

**University of Miami, Miller School of Medicine – Department of Neurology**\
*Research Support Specialist*

-   Conducted statistical analyses using SPSS and SAS on stroke-related datasets\
-   Designed and managed REDCap databases and data collection workflows\
-   Coordinated data-sharing efforts with hospitals across Florida to support statewide stroke initiatives\
-   Collaborated with multidisciplinary teams to support clinical and public health research

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Professional Strengths

My experience reflects a strong combination of domain expertise and technical skill, allowing me to bridge public health, data science, and real-world application.

-   Translating complex data into clear, actionable insights\
-   Applying rigorous epidemiologic methods within modern data science frameworks\
-   Working across clinical, research, and population health data environments\
-   Designing reproducible, scalable workflows for data analysis\
-   Communicating findings to both technical and non-technical audiences

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Career Direction

I am interested in opportunities that allow me to apply my expertise in health data science, real-world data, and predictive analytics to support decision-making in healthcare, public health, and industry settings. Let's connect if you are looking to collaborate.
